text.title: Trend Research Pipeline
text.summary: Three-stage pipeline for trend research: signal collection, scored triage, human checkpoint before writing deliverables.

section.title: Three-Stage Pipeline
section.p: Stage 1 (sonnet-class, broad): WebSearch + WebFetch across trend axes, collect raw signals. Stage 2 (opus-class, synthesis): score each signal on recency, evidence, and applicability; emit ranked table. Stage 3 (human checkpoint): accept or reject claims before they land in .aidocs/product_docs/trend-report.md.

rule.statement: The human checkpoint before writing to .aidocs/ is mandatory. Hallucinated tool names or vendor capabilities must not reach deliverables.
rule.rationale: Trend deliverables shape roadmap conversations. A hallucinated vendor capability or unsourced claim landing in a product brief costs more to correct downstream than the checkpoint costs upfront.

section.title: Signal Scoring
section.p: Three scoring dimensions, each 0-3, threshold for inclusion: total >= 5.

list.item: Recency: 3 = within 30 days, 2 = 31-90 days, 1 = 91-180 days, 0 = older than 180 days
Evidence: 3 = primary source, 2 = 3+ independent citations, 1 = secondary aggregator
Applicability: 0-3 set by human reviewer based on fit to this specific product

section.reference: Signal scoring script with threshold filtering and markdown table output

rule.statement: Persist raw signals to .aidocs/research/raw/signals.jsonl. The diff between this quarter and the previous file is more useful than the polished narrative.
rule.rationale: The trend delta — what is new, what faded, what reversed — is the real finding. Without a persistent artifact, each quarterly run starts from scratch and misses the directional signal.

rule.statement: Include a "kill list": for every trend adopted, name one prior practice stopped. Trend reports without subtractions are theatre.
rule.rationale: Additive-only trend reports imply infinite capacity. A kill list forces a real trade-off and signals to the team that the methodology acknowledges opportunity cost.
